name: Strimzi
what_it_does: Kubernetes operator for running Apache Kafka that manages clusters, topics, users, and connectors via Kubernetes CRDs and exposes a REST bridge for producing and consuming messages.
use_cases: Deploy and manage production Kafka clusters on Kubernetes using declarative CRD manifests
Manage Kafka topics and ACL-based user permissions through Kubernetes-native kubectl workflows
Produce and consume Kafka messages over HTTP using the Kafka Bridge without native Kafka client libraries
Run Kafka Connect and MirrorMaker 2 clusters managed as Kubernetes custom resources
not_for: Teams not running Kubernetes who need a managed Kafka service without infrastructure management
Simple message queue use cases where a lighter-weight broker such as RabbitMQ or NATS suffices
best_when: Best when platform teams need full control over Kafka deployments on Kubernetes without relying on a managed cloud Kafka service.
avoid_when: Avoid when your team lacks Kubernetes expertise or prefers a fully managed Kafka service such as Confluent Cloud or Amazon MSK.

auth.notes: Kafka Bridge REST API supports OAuth2 bearer tokens. Kafka cluster supports mTLS, SCRAM-SHA-512, and OAuth2 for client authentication. Kubernetes RBAC governs CRD access.

pricing.paid_tiers: Red Hat AMQ Streams is the supported enterprise distribution with paid support contracts
pricing.notes: Open-source and free to run. Enterprise support available from Red Hat. Infrastructure costs are your own.

performance.rate_limits: No platform-imposed rate limits; throughput depends on hardware and Kafka cluster configuration

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Kubernetes operator errors surface through CRD status conditions and pod logs; Kafka Bridge returns standard HTTP errors. Debugging requires kubectl familiarity.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: CRD-based management is fully declarative and idempotent; applying the same manifest is safe. Kafka Bridge message production is not idempotent without producer configuration.
known_agent_gotchas: Kafka Bridge must be explicitly deployed as a separate resource; it is not enabled by default on Kafka clusters
CRD schema versions evolve; agents must handle API version migrations when upgrading Strimzi
Consumer group management via Bridge requires creating a consumer instance before polling, which is stateful and session-bound
Kafka Bridge consumer sessions are not persistent; agents must re-create consumers after restarts

security_score_components.security_notes: Supports mTLS, SCRAM, and OAuth2; highly configurable security. Kubernetes RBAC adds another control layer. Open-source so dependency hygiene is community-verified.
